metadata._note: Centralized fiat-backed stablecoin (US T-bills via First Digital Trust). Apr-2-2025 confidence run on an insolvency allegation; secondary price dipped to ~0.87 then recovered. No on-chain backing failure: total supply stayed ~2.34B across the event.

Representative isolated FDUSD/USDC collateral market (LT 0.91, market-price FDUSD/USD oracle). A fund lending against FDUSD would liquidate once the secondary price falls below the LT. Modeled as a MEDIUM-confidence exposure edge: the dominant FDUSD risk was secondary-market price, but any FDUSD-collateralized loan is liquidated at 0.87.
FDUSD secondary-market DEX liquidity. No liquidation path (no LT) → a price wobble is a real but non-catastrophic DEPEG (WARN), not an imminent-liquidation CRITICAL.

oracle.oracle_provider: FDUSD/USD market-price oracle (tracks the real secondary discount, NOT a hardcoded 1.0)

meta.data_source: Representative FDUSD/USDC isolated collateral market, LT 0.91, market-price FDUSD/USD oracle — the regime where a fund's FDUSD-collateralized loan liquidates once secondary price < LT.
FDUSD secondary DEX liquidity, no liquidation path — a price wobble is a real WARN-grade exposure event, not a CRITICAL liquidation.

_invariant: FDUSD (First Digital USD) real on-chain supply 2341751540 @ block 22181387 (2025-04-02T12:59:59Z, 18dp, Alchemy archive). Confidence-run depeg: Justin Sun alleged First Digital Trust insolvency on Apr 2 2025; FDUSD fell to ~0.87 on secondary markets within hours, then recovered to ~0.99 by Apr 3. Backing (US T-bills) was CONTESTED publicly but NEVER impaired on-chain — supply moved <1% over the surrounding 2 days, so NO unbacked/supply detector should fire; only the price DEPEG is real. peg=1.0 USD; depeg price injected per-case.
